channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man killed in early morning shooting in Lidcombe, Sydney
item.description: In an early morning incident in Lidcombe, a suburb in western Sydney, a man was fatally shot. The attack occurred before dawn, and emergency services were promptly called to the scene. Authorities are...

item.title: All Six Crew Members Identified After US Refueling Plane Crash in Iraq
item.description: The identities of all six US military personnel who died in a refueling plane crash over western Iraq on March 12, 2026, have been released. The Boeing KC-135 aircraft, operating in friendly airspace,...

item.title: Investigation under way after newborn baby girl falls to her death in Westminster
item.description: Tragic events unfolded on Saturday morning in central London when an 18-day-old baby girl fell from a third-floor window of a building in the Peabody Estate on Horseferry Road, Westminster. Emergency ...

item.title: Nigeria Receives Full PSC Revenue Following Implementation of Tinubu’s Executive Order
item.description: In February 2026, Nigeria’s Federation Account finally received full remittances from Production Sharing Contracts (PSC) thanks to President Bola Tinubu’s Executive Order 9, which was signed earlier i...

item.title: Poilievre introduces plan to boost Canadian auto production and harmonize trade with the U.S.
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre has announced a comprehensive plan aimed at strengthening Canada's automotive sector while seeking tariff-free access to the U.S. market. Presented in Windsor, Ont...

item.title: Government to regulate medical costs to make healthcare affordable
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania government, through the Ministry of Health, is finalizing new guidelines aimed at lowering the cost of medicines, medical procedures, and treatment, starting with public hospi...

item.title: South Africa Seeks Accountability at ICJ Over Alleged Genocide in Gaza
item.description: South Africa has moved closer to resolving its genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice (ICJ), following Israel’s formal submission of its written response. The case, lodged ...

item.title: Holly Ramsay Supports Husband Adam Peaty at Edinburgh Swim Meet Amid Family Tensions
item.description: Holly Ramsay attended the International Swim Meet in Edinburgh on Sunday to support her husband, Olympic swimmer Adam Peaty, during his first major long-course competition since the Paris 2024 Olympic...

item.title: British Military Base in Nanyuki Continues Despite Kenya’s Independence
item.description: In Laikipia, the British Army Training Unit Kenya (BATUK) continues to operate on land historically linked to the Mau Mau uprising, decades after Kenya gained independence. The Mau Mau rebellion, whic...

item.title: Quezon City records lowest temperature since 2015 amid weakening monsoon
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Quezon City experienced a notably cool day yesterday as the temperature dropped to 17.1 degrees Celsius at the Science Garden, marking the lowest recorded temperature in the city...

item.title: Police seek identification of man found deceased in Coventry bin
item.description: West Midlands Police have appealed for the public's help in identifying a man found dead in a wheelie bin at Cash's Park, Coventry, on Friday at around 17:00 GMT. The man, estimated to be between 40 a...

item.title: Pakistani militant killed during foiled infiltration in Uri sector
item.description: In a significant security operation in J&K's Uri sector, Indian Army and police successfully thwarted an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control (LoC) early Sunday. Acting on specific intellige...

item.title: Government launches emergency response after Nairobi hit by heavy floods
item.description: Nairobi experienced another heavy downpour on Saturday night, a week after deadly floods caused loss of life and displacement. The rain led to flooding in multiple areas including Parklands, CBD, Ngon...

item.title: Government Registers 700-Metre Tunnel at Mweka College as Tourist Site
item.description: The Tanzanian government has officially added a 700-metre-long tunnel at Mweka College of African Wildlife Management in Kilimanjaro Region to the list of tourist attractions. The tunnel, which dates ...

item.title: Jozi My Jozi: Civic Initiatives Strengthening Pride and Participation in Johannesburg
item.description: Jozi My Jozi is a civic organisation aiming to revive pride and hope in Johannesburg's inner city through practical, visible interventions. Led by Innocent Mabusela, a former Anglo American economic d...

item.title: Reports Suggest Iran's New Supreme Leader Underwent Emergency Surgery in Russia
item.description: Speculation continues over the whereabouts of Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, following his assassination on 28 February 2026. Kuwaiti me...

item.title: Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe Launches Mind Robotics Startup Focused on Practical Industrial AI Robots
item.description: Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has launched a new robotics company called Mind Robotics, which he founded in November 2025 as a separate entity from Rivian (though Rivian maintains a partnership a...

item.title: Lahrith Properties donates food items to Ekiti inmates during Ramadan
item.description: Na Lahrith Properties, one real estate company, don show compassion this Ramadan by donating food and other relief items to inmates for Ekiti State Correctional Centre. The CEO, Soliat Odunayo Salaude...

item.title: London hosts Al-Quds Day protests with pro-Palestinian march and counter-demonstration
item.description: On Sunday, hundreds gathered in London for the annual Al-Quds Day event, a pro-Palestinian demonstration that was banned by the government from marching due to security concerns related to support for...

item.title: Five terrorists neutralised in intelligence-based operation in Lakki Marwat
item.description: In Lakki Marwat district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, security forces conducted a precise intelligence-based operation on Sunday, resulting in the killing of five terrorists, the Inter-Services Public Relat...
